FilterConfig.description: Schema for a single entry in the ``filters`` section of a logging config.

name.description: The logger name prefix used by the default `logging.Filter`; only records from loggers at or below this name pass through.
name.title: Name

().description: A custom factory (callable or dotted import path) used to construct this filter instead of the default `logging.Filter`. Any additional keys in this object are passed to the factory as keyword arguments.

definition.description: Base64-encoded cloudpickle payload of a factory callable used to construct this filter, taking precedence over `()`. Only honoured when the `logging_allow_pickled_definitions` setting is enabled.
definition.title: Definition

FilterConfig.title: FilterConfig

FormatterConfig.description: Schema for a single entry in the ``formatters`` section of a logging config.

class.description: Dotted import path to a `logging.Formatter` subclass (or any callable returning a formatter-like object) used to construct this formatter. If omitted, `logging.Formatter` is used.
class.title: Class

format.description: The format string passed as the `fmt` argument to the formatter constructor, e.g. `'%(levelname)s:%(name)s:%(message)s'`.
format.title: Format

datefmt.description: The `strftime`-style date/time format string used to render `%(asctime)s` in log records.
datefmt.title: Datefmt

style.description: The format-string style: `'%'` for printf-style, `'{'` for `str.format`-style, or `'$'` for `string.Template`-style.
style.title: Style

validate.description: If `True` (the default in `logging.Formatter`), validate that `format` is compatible with the selected `style` at construction time.
validate.title: Validate

defaults.description: A mapping of default values substituted for missing log record fields referenced in `format`.
defaults.title: Defaults

().description: A custom factory (callable or dotted import path) used to construct this formatter instead of `class_`/`logging.Formatter`. Any additional keys in this object are passed to the factory as keyword arguments.

definition.description: Base64-encoded cloudpickle payload of a factory callable used to construct this formatter, taking precedence over `class_` and `()`. Only honoured when the `logging_allow_pickled_definitions` setting is enabled.
definition.title: Definition

FormatterConfig.title: FormatterConfig

HandlerConfig.description: Schema for a single entry in the ``handlers`` section of a logging config.

class.description: Dotted import path to a `logging.Handler` subclass used to construct this handler, e.g. `'logging.StreamHandler'`.
class.title: Class

().description: A custom factory (callable or dotted import path) used to construct this handler instead of `class_`. Any additional keys in this object are passed to the factory as keyword arguments.

definition.description: Base64-encoded cloudpickle payload of a factory callable used to construct this handler, taking precedence over `class_` and `()`. Only honoured when the `logging_allow_pickled_definitions` setting is enabled.
definition.title: Definition

formatter.description: The name of an entry in the top-level `formatters` section to attach to this handler.
formatter.title: Formatter

level.description: The minimum log level (name such as `'INFO'`, or numeric value) this handler will process.
level.title: Level

filters.description: Names of entries in the top-level `filters` section to attach to this handler, or a converter-protocol string (e.g. `'runtime://...'`) resolving to such a list.
filters.title: Filters

target.description: The name of another entry in the top-level `handlers` section to use as the target, e.g. for `logging.handlers.MemoryHandler`.
target.title: Target

HandlerConfig.title: HandlerConfig

LoggerConfig.description: Schema for a single entry in the ``loggers`` section of a logging config.

level.description: The minimum log level (name such as `'DEBUG'`, or numeric value) this logger will process.
level.title: Level

propagate.description: Whether log records handled by this logger should also propagate to ancestor loggers.
propagate.title: Propagate

filters.description: Names of entries in the top-level `filters` section to attach to this logger, or a converter-protocol string (e.g. `'runtime://...'`) resolving to such a list.
filters.title: Filters

handlers.description: Names of entries in the top-level `handlers` section to attach to this logger, or a converter-protocol string (e.g. `'runtime://...'`) resolving to such a list.
handlers.title: Handlers

LoggerConfig.title: LoggerConfig

RootLoggerConfig.description: Schema for the ``root`` section of a logging config.

__merge__.description: Internal merge directive consumed by `merge_configs`. When set to `'deep'`, this entry is recursively field-merged into the corresponding base entry instead of replacing it wholesale. Stripped before the config is applied.
__merge__.title: Merge

level.description: The minimum log level (name such as `'WARNING'`, or numeric value) the root logger will process.
level.title: Level

filters.description: Names of entries in the top-level `filters` section to attach to the root logger, or a converter-protocol string (e.g. `'runtime://...'`) resolving to such a list.
filters.title: Filters

handlers.description: Names of entries in the top-level `handlers` section to attach to the root logger, or a converter-protocol string (e.g. `'runtime://...'`) resolving to such a list.
handlers.title: Handlers

RootLoggerConfig.title: RootLoggerConfig

description: Top-level schema for a dict-based logging configuration.

version.description: Schema version identifier. Must be `1`; this is the only version currently supported.
version.title: Version

formatters.description: A mapping of formatter name to its configuration, defining the layout of log messages.
formatters.title: Formatters

filters.description: A mapping of filter name to its configuration, used to selectively allow/deny log records.
filters.title: Filters

handlers.description: A mapping of handler name to its configuration, defining where log records are emitted to.
handlers.title: Handlers

loggers.description: A mapping of logger name to its configuration, for configuring non-root loggers.
loggers.title: Loggers

incremental.description: If `True`, this configuration only updates handler levels and logger levels/propagation for already-configured objects, rather than performing a full reconfiguration.
incremental.title: Incremental

disable_existing_loggers.description: If `True` (the default), loggers not present in this configuration that existed prior to configuring are disabled (unless they are children of a logger that is present).
disable_existing_loggers.title: Disable Existing Loggers

title: LoggingConfigModel
